tests.description: Repeated agent delegation with identical signature must be stopped by Runtime Guard.
Individually plausible model/tool steps must stop once cumulative budget is exceeded.
A valid capability token must not override an exhausted Runtime Guard budget.

decision.reason: Read-only local file analysis is allowed when no higher risk applies.

governance_decisions.reason: Read-only local file analysis is allowed when no higher risk applies.

tests.description: Enforcement must reject actions without a capability token and write audit evidence.

decision.reason: Read-only local file analysis is allowed when no higher risk applies.

governance_decisions.reason: Read-only local file analysis is allowed when no higher risk applies.

tests.description: Pattern Engine may create proposals from repeated rejections but must not mutate rules.

proposals.reason: Repeated enforcement rejections for capability='write_files', bucket='missing_token'.
proposals.proposed_change: Review whether task prompts, capability requests, or rules should be clarified. Do not auto-change rules.

tests.description: Repeated identical governance evaluation must not corrupt audit evidence.
notes: Current behavior deduplicates governance audit events; it does not implement semantic no-progress stopping.

decision.reason: Read-only local file analysis is allowed when no higher risk applies.

governance_decisions.reason: Read-only local file analysis is allowed when no higher risk applies.
Read-only local file analysis is allowed when no higher risk applies.

tests.description: Alternating multi-agent delegation with changing signatures must still stop at the task step budget.
Runtime Guard must fail closed when no runtime budget exists for a task.
Exact budget consumption is allowed; the first step beyond budget must stop with evidence.
Documents current scope: cost budget is enforced per task; no global/session budget is implemented in core runtime.
notes: Known remaining gap: there is no cumulative session/global budget across multiple tasks in current core scope.

tests.description: Twenty individually allowed tasks accumulate cost without a session/global budget stop.
notes: This is an evidence finding, not a core bug: per-task budgets work, but cumulative cross-task drift is not blocked.
Use this as the proof basis before adding session/global budgets.

tests.description: Three agents can each stay under per-task budgets while aggregate session cost rises.
notes: Evidence confirms missing aggregate budget across agents.
Core invariants remain intact; the gap is economic/runtime aggregation, not token enforcement.

tests.description: A logical continuation can receive a fresh per-task budget after task/session reset.
notes: This documents the strongest current cost-control gap: per-task reset can bypass cumulative intent/session cost limits.
No core invariant is violated; missing concept is logical session/global budget continuity.

tests.description: One hundred individually valid micro-tasks create aggregate cost without a global stop.
notes: Evidence confirms that micro-task flooding is visible in logs but not blocked by current per-task runtime budgets.
This finding should drive the next controlled design step: session/global budget policy, not Pattern Engine automation.
